name: Chest CT High-Resolution (HRCT)
description: High-resolution CT of the chest for evaluation of interstitial lung disease and diffuse pulmonary pathology

findings.technique: High-resolution CT of the chest was performed with 1-1.5 mm sections at 10 mm intervals through the lungs in inspiration and expiration.
findings.comparison: No prior studies available for comparison.

sections.name: Pattern Recognition
sections.content: There is [no reticular pattern / no ground-glass opacity / no nodular pattern / no cystic pattern] identified to suggest interstitial lung disease.

sections.name: Lung Parenchyma
sections.content: The lung parenchyma demonstrates [no honeycombing / no traction bronchiectasis / no architectural distortion]. The alveolar spaces are [clear / no consolidation].

sections.name: Distribution
sections.content: There is [no peripheral / no central / no upper lobe / no lower lobe] predominance of abnormalities.

sections.name: Bronchi and Bronchioles
sections.content: The airways demonstrate [no bronchial wall thickening / no bronchiectasis / no tree-in-bud opacities]. The bronchi are [patent / normal].

sections.name: Vessels
sections.content: The pulmonary vessels are [normal in caliber / no pulmonary arterial hypertension]. No embolic disease.

sections.name: Lymph Nodes
sections.content: The mediastinal and hilar lymph nodes are [not enlarged / no lymphadenopathy]. No abnormal lymph node enlargement.

sections.name: Pleura
sections.content: The bilateral pleura demonstrate [no thickening / no plaques / no calcification]. No pleural effusion.

sections.name: Expiratory Images
sections.content: The expiratory images demonstrate [no air-trapping / no mosaic attenuation]. No small airway disease.

impression:
1. Normal high-resolution chest CT.
2. No interstitial lung disease, ground-glass opacity, or honeycombing identified.
3. No evidence of small airway disease on expiratory images.
author: RadReport Templates
